title: Cannot call a class as a function in building app Next.Js 13
author: arjay-dala
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

All seems working in development but when I try to build my Next.Js app I keep getting error:
"[Application error: a client-side exception has occurred (see the browser console for more information)](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/77293030/nextjs-application-error-a-client-side-exception-has-occurred-see-the-browser)"

When I check the console in browser it says,
"Uncaught (in promise) TypeError: Cannot call a class as a function".

Upon investigating, it points out to this line of code:
const wb = new ExcelJS.Workbook()

Is there anyone can help with this issue?

comments.author: PsHohe
comments.body: I'm facing the same issue. It seems to be the same as this: https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/issues/2549#issue-1929946633

comments.author: brayo333
comments.body: I was using next 13.5.3, downgrading to next 13.3.1 seemed to have fixed this for me

comments.author: PsHohe
comments.body: In my case, I fixed it by using a library called react-use-exceljs, which is a wrapper around exceljs, instead of importing exceljs directly in the page. It works with 13.5.3.

comments.author: developenguin
comments.body:
We had this in our NextJS app as wel, in a utility function.
Doing `const ExcelJS = await import('exceljs');` seems to have fixed it for us.
